text: **The fix:** `who --all-projects` fell back to the old `WhoSnapshot`/`render_who_*` markdown-table renderer because the daemon's `rpc_who` only builds the unified fabric view when a single project scope resolves — `--all-projects` has no single scope, so that branch was skipped entirely (this was a known, documented open tail from the 2026-06-29 session that unified `who` with hook injection). I added `render_fabric_all_projects`/`_human` in `fabric_context.rs` (one project block per root channel, roster rendered once up front) and wired it into the `all_projects` branch of `rpc_who`. Compiled clean, clippy clean, all existing `who`/`fabric_context` unit tests pass, and a new isolated integration test confirms the unified format renders (still finishing a wrinkle in that test around session-id anchoring for the second project). [...] **Fixed:** `who --all-projects` now renders through the same unified fabric pipeline (`build_view` → `render_view`/`render_human_view`) as single-project `who` and hook injection, instead of falling back to the old markdown-table `WhoSnapshot` renderer. Added `render_fabric_all_projects`/`_human` in `fabric_context.rs` (one project block per root channel, invitable-agent roster shown once up front since it's scope-independent), wired into the `all_projects` branch of `rpc_who`. Added a regression test (`who_all_projects_uses_unified_fabric_render_not_old_table`) proving the old table format is gone.
